Operational Incident Report — Shanbei Quanta Networks Co., Ltd. (USCC 91239900MA0T9P0QX), a Shenzhen Innovators Board issuer, internal reference SQN-OPS-2025-0612-01.
On 2025-06-12 10:34 CST, a misconfiguration in the HR onboarding form within the “Qiyu Hire” portal briefly allowed authenticated line managers to view two additional fields (full name and partially masked resident ID) for 42 new hires during a role mapping update by the IT admin team.
Exposed data categories were limited to names, corporate emails, PRC mobile numbers, and resident ID numbers with middle digits masked; no bank accounts, medical data, or minors under 14 were present, and the exposure window lasted 23 minutes before deactivation.
No unapproved cross-border transfer occurred; however, CloudCedar Pte. Ltd. (Singapore) is our backup vendor under DPA Schedule 2 (HR Data), and the relevant data-export approval (Haitang Municipal Cyberspace Administration Standard Contract Filing Ref: SCF-2025-HT-1173) remains in effect for hashed personnel identifiers only.
We received one DSAR from employee code E-2025-044 at 13:05 CST requesting confirmation of processing and log extracts; we responded within 48 hours with minimised content (hashes and timestamps only) and withheld administrator IP addresses per internal security exception protocol.
Two employees (E-2025-037 and E-2025-041) withdrew consent for overseas backup at 14:22 CST; export of their records was ceased immediately, mirrored snapshots were replaced with null tokens, and an addendum to the Standard Contract was executed to exclude their identifiers.
Erasure actions completed at 15:10 CST included deletion of temporary cache files on the application server and purge of hourly backups; our retention schedule remains 90 days for routine HR data, with a legal hold of 6 months on incident audit logs pursuant to PIPL obligations.
As an issuer, we assessed potential MNPI exposure because a pre-disclosure draft of the Q2 revenue schedule was stored in the same network drive; access logs show the MNPI file was not opened during the incident, and no inside information was transmitted externally during the closed trading window.
A voluntary compliance memo was filed with the Jingnan Provincial Securities Supervision Bureau within 24 hours, and we concluded this event does not constitute a material information disclosure under applicable listing rules of the Shenzhen Innovators Board.
Per DPA Schedule 2, only salted hashes of resident IDs may be processed offshore; our policy prohibits storing raw resident IDs outside the Mainland, and an audit confirmed no offshore copies—note that a draft “Data Export Register” entry existed but was not activated.
Employment onboarding workflows have been revised to collect a minimal fieldset (name, start date, and last four digits of resident ID only), and the consent dialog now includes a clear toggle for optional overseas backup with a record of choices stored in the consent ledger.
Role-based access controls were re-applied and verified at 16:00 CST, replacing the misassigned “manager_plus” role with a least-privilege “manager_view_basic” role, and privileged access requests now require dual approval.
For cross-border governance, the Data Protection Office will complete a quarterly revalidation of the SCF-2025-HT-1173 filing and maintain the Record of Processing Activities to reflect consent withdrawals and erasures completed on 2025-06-12.
Point of contact: Data Protection Officer, Ms. Zhu Wen, dpo@shanbei-quanta.cn, +86-21-6100-5520; employee queries may also be sent to hr-privacy@shanbei-quanta.cn.
This report contains no customer account numbers and does not request any verification action; testing artifacts in the staging environment mentioned herein are already decommissioned and should not be treated as a current risk.
